claim_bullets.bullet_text: To make pairs work, we did things backwards, we passed the first and rest functions to the pair, and the pair called our function (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.189-190).

But we could have done something completely different; we could have written a pair that stored its elements in an array, or a pair that stored its elements in a POJO (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.189-190).
We could have written a pair that stored its elements in an array, or a pair that stored its elements in a POJO (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.189-190).

source_record_text: Chapter on backward thinking in JavaScript allonge, discussing pairs and lists, and how implementation details can be hidden using functional patterns.
